WELCOME TO ACE BELÉM
WELCOME FROM THE ACE COMMITTEE
BELÉM OVERVIEW
WHAT SETS BELÉM APART?
PRIORITY AREAS
ACE BELÉM SITES
ACE BELÉM DELEGATION
STAY CONNECTED

Ladies and Gentlemen,
It is with great pleasure that the Government of Pará welcomes the 21st 
edition of the Americas Competitiveness Exchange (ACE) to Belém — a 
gathering of such significance for hemispheric integration.
We are honored by the opportunity to present the very best our state 
has to offer to our continent.
Pará, the second largest state in Brazil by territorial area, has been gaining 
national and international prominence. We are the leading economy in 
the Northern region of Brazil and rank among the fastest-growing states 
in the country. In November of this year, global attention will be focused 
on our state and on our capital, Belém, as we host COP30 — the most 
important global event on climate change.
It is within this dynamic and modern economic environment that we 
will seek to immerse participants during ACE Belém. To enrich this 
experience, we will welcome voices from across Brazil to engage in 
discussions on innovation, sustainable development, and public policy.
Throughout the program, ACE Belém participants will have the 
opportunity to visit universities, technology parks, successful local 
initiatives in the field of sustainable development, and witness a part of 
our long and rich history.
We have much to share. It will be a considerable challenge to convey a 
comprehensive view of such a diverse state within the span of just one 
week. But rest assured: no effort will be spared.
We look forward to welcoming you.
Helder Zahluth Barbalho
Governor of the State of Pará

WELCOME FROM 
THE ACE COMMITTEE
Dear distinguished leaders from the Americas and beyond,
We are honored to welcome you to the 21st edition of the Americas Competitiveness 
Exchange (ACE) Belém. ACE is a powerful mechanism that fosters cooperation 
and creation of new partnerships to strengthen economic development in the 
Americas.
ACE Belém is convened by the Organization of American States (OAS) and the 
Government of the State of Pará and the Ministry of External Relations of 
the Federative Republic of Brazil, with the support of the Government of the 
United States, through the Department of Commerce - Economic Development 
Administration, and the Department of State – Permanent Mission to the OAS.
Throughout your week in Belém, August 25 to 29, 2025, you will have the opportunity 
to engage with decision-makers from across the Americas and beyond to foster 
collaboration and partnerships with Belém’s research institutions, development 
programs, emerging innovation centers, technology parks, and leading companies 
across various sectors. Participants will also have the opportunity to interact with 
local leaders from Belém’s main industries, gaining insights into their respective 
clusters, technological advancements, and collaboration opportunities.
We want to emphasize the importance of nurturing the connections that you 
establish over the course of this week to ensure you make the most of your time 
in ACE. We are excited for what awaits us in Belém and the connections that 
will emerge from this week. To facilitate meaningful and strategic engagement 
throughout the program, we have compiled this Tripbook to share key information 
about the ACE Belém agenda and participants. We encourage you to use this 
resource as a compass to identify potential business partners for trade deals, 
research and development, new ventures, soft-landing opportunities, technical 
exchanges, and any other collaboration. 
The ACE Committee wishes to express its most sincere gratitude to Government of 
the State of Pará, for the exceptional agenda they have prepared for this important 
exchange in collaboration with other key stakeholders. 
We look forward to our work together.
Sincerely,
The ACE Committee
Americas
Competitiveness
Exchange

5
BELÉM
OVERVIEW
WHAT SETS BELÉM APART?
Belém, the capital of the state of Pará, is one of the most emblematic cities in 
Brazil. With its lush nature and strategic location in the heart of the Amazon 
rainforest, it plays an important role in the country’s culture, economy and 
tourism. The city is a pivotal hub for the sale of products such as açaí, Brazil 
nuts, and cocoa, in addition to being an important center for industry and 
services. 
Belém and Pará are making tireless efforts to preserve the environment, keep 
the Amazon standing, add value to what they produce through education, 
scientific research and innovation, as well as to ensure the quality of life for 
30 million people of the Amazon. 
 
 Belém is the gateway to the Amazon region. 
 Diversified and growing economy.
 Important center of industry and services.
 National reference in environmental preservation and sustainability.

PRIORITY 
AREAS

7
ACE BELÉM 
SITES
A historic colonial mansion and cultural center located in 
Belém, Pará. It offers an opportunity to discover the region’s 
past, architectural heritage, and the vibrant landscape of 
contemporary Brazilian art. Overlooking the Guajará Bay, it also 
provides a unique setting to explore the cultural intersections 
of the Amazon—both past and present.
The Brazilian Agricultural Research Corporation (Embrapa) is 
the leading agricultural in Brazil. Embrapa is one of the largest 
agricultural research corporations of the world, with over half a 
century of contributions focused on innovation, efficiency, and 
sustainability.
Embrapa has a presence in several countries through its Labex, 
virtual laboratories which facilitate research collaborations and 
knowledge exchange with international partners and offices. 
There are Labex in the United States, France, Netherlands, and 
the United Kingdom, South Korea, among other countries.
Embrapa Amazônia Oriental
Casa das Onze Janelas

8
Established in 2014, holds a series of companies with bioeconomy 
assets at a global scale. The Brazilian company Natura is the anchor 
of the industrial complex that combines innovative companies, 
respect for natural habitats, engagement of the the local 
community, and collaboration among private organizations. 
Is the largest Brazilian cosmetics company by revenue. The Natura 
& Co Group is present in 73 countries across all continents. Natura’s 
mission combines promoting a beauty experience rooted in the 
delicate balance between nature and science. Their innovative 
formulas combine the biodiversity of the Amazon Forest with the 
latest, greatest cosmetic technology in all their products.
With more than 70 years of history, FIEPA is the main representative 
of Pará’s industrial sector, serving as a platform to promote 
competitiveness, innovation, and sustainable development. FIEPA 
operate several key institutions that jointly support industrial growth 
through education, social development, internationalization, and 
business intelligence. 
 National Service for Industrial Learning (SENAI): Provides 
technical education and professional training to support the 
qualification of the industrial workforce.
 Social Service of Industry (SESI): Promotes workers’ quality of life 
through initiatives in health, education, and workplace safety.
 Euvaldo Lodi Institute (IEL): Encourages innovation and 
entrepreneurship by connecting academia and industry.
 International Business Center (CIN): Supports companies in 
foreign trade and global market integration.
 Innovation and Economic Sustainability - REDES: Strengthens 
local value chains through supplier development and sustainable 
business practices.
 Credit Access Center (NAC): Offers technical guidance and 
support for companies seeking financing.
Federação das Indústrias 
do Estado do Pará
Ecoparque Benevides
Natura & Co Holding S.A.

9
Located at Praça da República in downtown Belém, Teatro 
Waldemar Henrique is notable for its versatility, accommodating 
both proscenium (Italian-style) and arena stage formats. Featuring 
eclectic architecture with elements of Art Nouveau, the theater 
emerged from the local cultural movement’s efforts to create a 
space for more innovative and experimental artistic expressions.
The theater will host a panel on the Brazilian Innovation Ecosystem 
and the Innovation Showcase.
The Guamá Science and Technology Park stands out for its 
activities in the areas of Information Technology, Biotechnology, 
Bioeconomy, Energy, and Mining. With more than 30 resident 
companies, 
40 
associates, 
12 
research 
and 
development 
laboratories, as well as a technical school. This collaboration 
enables a vibrant and dynamic environment that encourages 
applied research, innovative and sustainable entrepreneurship, 
and the generation of solutions for local and global challenges.
Services offered include: 
a) The iConnect network, that brings together independent 
professionals, research and development laboratories and the 
industrial sector; 
b) export training programm PEIEX that increases the international 
competitiveness of Brazilian companies, and services in innovation 
management, business planning, competitive intelligence and 
support for technology transfer.
Universidade Federal do Pará
Teatro Waldemar Henrique
Parque de Ciência e 
Tecnologia do Guamá (PCT)
The Universidade Federal do Pará (UFPA) is the largest public 
university in the Amazon region with more than 50.000 
students and 3.000 faculty members across 12 campuses across 
multiple cities in the state of Pará. UFPA is a national reference 
in research and innovation. Some of its stand out fields of 
study include: biomedical sciences, parasitology, tropical 
diseases and geosciences. UFPA also excels on environmental, 
social science, engineering, computer science and artificial 
intelligence.

10
Located on Combu Island in Belém, Casa de Chocolate Dona Nena 
carries out sustainable projects that preserve the forest and honor 
the ancestral knowledge of Amazonian peoples. A successful 
example of the bioeconomy in practice, the company also offers 
guided tours allowing visitors to experience local chocolate 
production in the heart of the Amazon rainforest.
Teatro Gasômetro is situated within a botanical park and features a 
distinctive architectural design. The seating area includes a central 
location surrounded by concrete bleachers, framed by openwork 
iron structures and a glass dome that lend the venue an elegant 
and unique ambiance.
This space will serve as main site for the final panel on small and 
medium enterprises and closin ceremony of ACE Belém.
Usina da Paz
Casa de Chocolate Dona Nena
Gasômetro
The “Usina da Paz” are multifunctional complexes with the aim of 
promoting peace, citizenship and social inclusion. These centers 
have delivered over 9.7 million citizen services to date including 
healthcare, 
education, 
cultural 
programming, 
sports, 
and 
professional development. All services are offered free of charge. 
Since the first Usina da Paz opened in 2021, more than 14 units 
have become fully operational in Pará and other cities.
